lesson: Reboots only at peak service, with the terminal voltage sagging to 196 V, mean an undersized supply cable. It is an electrician’s problem wearing a machine-fault disguise.
symptoms: Machine randomly reboots during busy periods.
Only happens when the kitchen is really flat out.

tests.error-log: Power reset events logged at peak service times — 12:10, 12:43, 13:02 today.
tests.continuity-test: Supply voltage at the machine terminal measures 196 V during a reset event — spec is 220–240 V. Supply cable run is undersized.

flavour: The machine wasn't faulty. The electrician was.
authenticityNote: An undersized supply cable causes voltage sag when the compressor starts under load; the controller brownouts and reboots. An electrician's install problem wearing a machine-fault disguise.
